# AlgoFlow — Master Plan

<!-- Generated by Maestro Planner Phase P0 -->
<!-- Last updated: 2026-03-23 after P0 audits (user-value + docs-sync) -->

## Project Overview
- **Name**: AlgoFlow
- **One-line Description**: Real-time programmable payroll streaming on Algorand — the first continuous salary disbursement protocol on the Algorand blockchain.
- **Vision Statement**: AlgoFlow reimagines payroll for the decentralized era. Instead of monthly lump-sum payments through banks and intermediaries, employers fund a smart contract with tokenized salary units that stream continuously to employees — accruing every second, withdrawable anytime, verifiable on-chain. Built on Algorand's instant finality and native asset primitives, AlgoFlow delivers what Ethereum-based streaming protocols (Sablier, Superfluid) cannot: atomic batch operations, sub-$0.001 fees, and delegated transaction authority via rekeying.
- **Hackathon**: Infinova Hackathon — Blockchain with Algorand track, Option 1

---

## Problem Statement

### Current State
Global organizations, DAOs, and remote teams (70%+ of Web3 hires are contractors) rely on traditional payroll systems that batch-process salaries monthly or bi-weekly. These systems require banks, payroll processors (ADP, Gusto), and intermediaries — adding delays (2-5 days), fees ($25-50 per wire), and opacity. Crypto-native organizations face additional pain:

- **Cross-border friction**: SWIFT transfers take 3-5 days, $25-50 per wire
- **Volatility risk**: Token value can swing between payment issuance and conversion
- **No programmability**: Cannot enforce on-chain vesting, milestone releases, or conditional bonuses
- **Contractor overhead**: 70%+ of Web3 workers are contractors — traditional payroll isn't designed for them
- **Transparency gap**: No real-time visibility into accrued earnings or audit trail

### Desired State
Employers lock funds into a transparent, auditable smart contract. Salaries stream continuously — employees see their earnings tick up in real-time and withdraw whenever they want. No intermediaries, no delays, instant settlement, and full programmability (pause, adjust rates, milestone payments, conditional streams).

### Why Now
- $390-400B in stablecoin payments processed in 2025
- 25%+ of multinationals now integrate crypto payroll
- Algorand Python and AlgoKit have matured — smart contract development is now accessible
- The Algorand payroll streaming space is essentially empty (no direct competitor)
- First-mover opportunity on a chain with superior technical characteristics

### Why Algorand (Not Ethereum)
Ethereum-based streaming protocols (Sablier: 552K+ streams, ~$250M TVL; Superfluid: 500+ projects) suffer from fundamental limitations that Algorand doesn't have:

| Limitation | Ethereum | Algorand |
|---|---|---|
| Finality | 12.8 minutes (2 epochs) | 3.3 seconds (instant, no reorgs) |
| Transaction cost | $2-$45 gas on L1 | $0.00024 fixed fee |
| Token streaming | Requires wrapping (Super Tokens) | ASAs stream natively — zero wrapping |
| Batch operations | No native atomic batching | 16-txn atomic groups (all-or-nothing) |
| Key management | No rekeying — address change = new stream | Native rekeying for delegated authority |
| Fee predictability | Variable — spikes during congestion | Fixed regardless of network load |

---

## Target Users

### Persona 1: DAO Treasury Manager ("Raj")
- **Role**: Manages treasury and contributor payments for a 50-person DAO
- **Goals**: Automate contributor payments, reduce operational overhead, provide real-time payment visibility to contributors
- **Pain Points**: Monthly batch payments cause cash flow issues for contributors; cross-border wires are expensive and slow; no audit trail beyond spreadsheets
- **Tech Savviness**: High — uses crypto wallets daily, understands blockchain concepts
- **Key workflows**: Fund contract → register employees → monitor streams → manage rates → handle departures
- **Value from AlgoFlow**: Eliminates payroll ops overhead, provides auditable on-chain records, enables programmable pay conditions

### Persona 2: Remote Contractor ("Alice")
- **Role**: Smart contract developer working for 2-3 DAOs simultaneously
- **Goals**: Get paid continuously for work done, withdraw funds when needed, track earnings across multiple engagements
- **Pain Points**: Waits 30+ days for payment; no visibility into accrued earnings; different payment systems for each client
- **Tech Savviness**: High — has Algorand wallet, comfortable signing transactions
- **Key workflows**: Connect wallet → view accrual → withdraw → check history → monitor rate changes
- **Value from AlgoFlow**: Real-time earnings visibility, instant withdrawal, on-chain proof of payment history

---

## Product Scope

### MVP (Must-Have for Hackathon Demo) — 42 FRs

#### Smart Contract Layer (14 FRs)
1. **12 ABI methods** in Algorand Python (algopy):
   - `create(asset)` — Initialize contract with salary ASA
   - `opt_in_asset()` — Contract opts into the salary ASA
   - `fund(axfer)` — Deposit salary tokens into contract
   - `register_employee(account, rate)` — Register employee with hourly rate
   - `withdraw()` — Employee claims all accrued salary tokens
   - `get_accrued(account)` — Read-only: view accrued balance
   - `update_rate(account, new_rate)` — Settle at old rate, apply new rate
   - `pause_stream(account)` — Settle accrued, then pause stream
   - `milestone_pay(employee, amount)` — One-time milestone payment via inner transaction
   - `resume_stream(account)` — Resume a paused stream
   - `remove_employee(account)` — Final payout + deregister employee
   - `pause_all()` — Emergency pause all active streams
2. **Authorization enforcement** — all employer methods validate `Txn.sender == self.employer`
3. **Overdraft protection** — contract checks balance before inner transfer; auto-pauses if insufficient

#### Token / ASA Layer (4 FRs)
4. **PAYUSD salary token** (ASA) — analogous to USDT, 1 PAYUSD = $1, 6 decimals, configurable supply
5. **Clawback configuration** — contract address set as ASA clawback for programmatic transfers
6. **Employee ASA opt-in** — flow for employees to opt into the salary token
7. **Employee app opt-in** — flow for employees to opt into the contract (local state allocation)

#### Employer Dashboard — `/employer` route (9 FRs)
8. **Wallet connection** — Pera Wallet (Testnet) / KMD (LocalNet) via `@txnlab/use-wallet-react`
9. **Fund contract** — deposit PAYUSD tokens with amount input and transaction confirmation
10. **Register employees** — enter Algorand address + hourly rate; batch register up to 3 atomically
11. **Employee list** — live view of all registered employees: address, rate, status, accrued amount
12. **Stream management** — pause, resume, update rate, remove employee from the list view
13. **Milestone payments** — "Send Milestone Payment" button per employee with amount input
14. **Emergency controls** — "Pause All Streams" button for emergency stop
15. **Contract health** — runway indicator (hours/days until depleted), low-fund warning banner when <24h
16. **Setup checklist** — first-time users see guided steps: deploy status, ASA opt-in, funding, first employee

#### Employee Dashboard — `/employee` route (5 FRs)
17. **Wallet connection** — same provider abstraction as employer
18. **Streaming counter** — real-time accrual counter ticking every second (the "wow" moment)
19. **Withdraw button** — one-click withdrawal of all accrued salary
20. **Transaction history** — past withdrawals with timestamps, amounts, and explorer links
21. **Rate & status display** — current rate in $/hr, $/day, $/week, $/month; active/paused indicator; "New" badge for changes since last visit

#### Shared Frontend (7 FRs)
22. **Role-based routing** — auto-detect employer from contract global state; redirect to correct dashboard
23. **Landing page** (`/`) — "How it Works" architecture diagram, role selection entry points
24. **Loading states** — spinners during wallet connection, transaction signing, confirmation waiting
25. **Error handling** — user-friendly messages for rejected transactions, insufficient funds, network errors
26. **Success confirmations** — toast notifications with tx ID and explorer link after every on-chain action
27. **Network badge** — indicator showing LocalNet or Testnet connection
28. **Explorer integration** — every transaction links to Lora Explorer (Testnet) or local equivalent

#### DevOps / Demo (4 FRs)
29. **Deployment script** — single-command deploy of ASA + contract to LocalNet or Testnet (`--network` flag)
30. **Account setup script** — fund test accounts with ALGO and PAYUSD tokens
31. **Automated demo script** — Python script executing full create→fund→register→stream→withdraw flow
32. **Demo reset script** — tear down and redeploy for clean state between demo runs

#### UX Polish (3 FRs)
33. **Dark theme with AlgoGate aesthetic** — glassmorphism cards, spotlight hover effects, Silk 3D background, shimmer text
34. **Timezone handling** — contract uses UTC; frontend converts to browser local timezone for all displayed times
35. **Multi-unit rate display** — employee rates shown as $/hr, $/day, $/week, $/month simultaneously

### Growth (Post-Hackathon / Stretch Goals) — 5 FRs
1. `resume_all()` — resume all paused streams (inverse of pause_all)
2. `drain_funds()` — employer emergency withdrawal of remaining contract balance
3. Export payroll report as CSV — downloadable audit trail
4. Rekeying demonstration — employer delegates to HR manager address
5. ARC-28 event emission — on-chain events for stream_started, withdrawal, rate_changed, stream_paused

### Vision (Long-Term Roadmap)
1. Multi-employer aggregation — employees see all streams across contracts in one view
2. Multi-asset streaming — salary + bonus tokens in the same contract
3. USDC stablecoin streaming — use Algorand-native USDC ASA instead of custom token
4. Rekeying-based DAO governance — multi-sig approval flows for payroll operations
5. AI-powered salary suggestions — market rate analysis, compliance flagging
6. Mobile app — push notifications for accrual milestones, rate changes
7. Cross-contract milestone system — complex deliverable tracking with conditional release

### Explicitly Out of Scope
1. Fiat on/off ramps — no bank integration
2. Tax calculation or compliance reporting
3. KYC/AML verification
4. Multi-chain deployment (Algorand only)
5. Production-grade security audit
6. Mobile wallet app development
7. Mainnet deployment (testnet only for hackathon)
8. Multi-employer aggregation view (acknowledged for Vision)
9. Offline push notifications (acknowledged for Vision)

## Smart Contract Specification

### Global State Schema
| Key | Type | Description | Set by |
|-----|------|-------------|--------|
| `employer` | Account | Address of the employer who created the contract | `create()` |
| `salary_asset` | Asset | ASA ID of the salary token (PAYUSD) | `create()` |
| `total_employees` | UInt64 | Count of currently registered employees | `register_employee()`, `remove_employee()` |
| `total_streamed` | UInt64 | Cumulative tokens disbursed to all employees (lifetime) | `withdraw()`, `milestone_pay()`, `remove_employee()` |
| `is_paused` | UInt64 | Global pause flag (0 = active, 1 = paused) | `pause_all()`, `resume_all()` |

### Local State Schema (Per Employee)
| Key | Type | Description | Set by |
|-----|------|-------------|--------|
| `salary_rate` | UInt64 | Tokens per hour in base units (6 decimals) | `register_employee()`, `update_rate()` |
| `stream_start` | UInt64 | Unix timestamp when streaming began | `register_employee()`, `resume_stream()` |
| `last_withdrawal` | UInt64 | Unix timestamp of most recent withdrawal | `withdraw()`, `update_rate()`, `pause_stream()` |
| `total_withdrawn` | UInt64 | Cumulative tokens withdrawn by this employee | `withdraw()` |
| `is_active` | UInt64 | Stream status: 0 = paused/removed, 1 = active | `register_employee()`, `pause_stream()`, `resume_stream()` |

### Method Specifications

#### `create(asset: Asset) → None` [create="require"]
- **Caller**: Employer (contract deployer)
- **Auth**: None (only callable at creation)
- **Behavior**: Sets employer address, salary asset ID, initializes counters to 0
- **State changes**: employer, salary_asset, total_employees, total_streamed, is_paused

#### `opt_in_asset() → None`
- **Caller**: Employer
- **Auth**: `Txn.sender == self.employer`
- **Behavior**: Contract opts into the salary ASA via inner transaction so it can hold tokens
- **Prerequisites**: Contract must have enough ALGO for MBR (0.1 ALGO per ASA opt-in)

#### `fund(axfer: AssetTransferTxn) → None`
- **Caller**: Employer
- **Auth**: `Txn.sender == self.employer`
- **Behavior**: Accepts an asset transfer of PAYUSD tokens into the contract. Grouped atomically with the app call.
- **Validation**: Asset transfer must be for the correct ASA ID and to the contract address

#### `register_employee(employee: Account, rate: UInt64) → None`
- **Caller**: Employer
- **Auth**: `Txn.sender == self.employer`
- **Behavior**: Sets employee's local state: salary_rate, stream_start (now), last_withdrawal (now), total_withdrawn (0), is_active (1). Increments total_employees.
- **Prerequisites**: Employee must have opted into both the ASA and the app
- **Validation**: Employee must not already be registered (rate == 0 check)

#### `withdraw() → UInt64`
- **Caller**: Employee (any opted-in account)
- **Auth**: Caller must have is_active == 1
- **Behavior**: Calculates accrued = `rate * (now - last_withdrawal) / 3600`. Checks contract balance (overdraft protection). Sends tokens via inner AssetTransfer. Updates last_withdrawal. Returns amount withdrawn.
- **Overdraft**: If contract balance < accrued, sends available balance and pauses the stream
- **Returns**: Amount of tokens transferred

#### `get_accrued(employee: Account) → UInt64` [readonly=True]
- **Caller**: Anyone
- **Auth**: None
- **Behavior**: Pure calculation: `rate * (now - last_withdrawal) / 3600`. Does not modify state.
- **Returns**: Accrued token amount (not yet withdrawn)

#### `update_rate(employee: Account, new_rate: UInt64) → None`
- **Caller**: Employer
- **Auth**: `Txn.sender == self.employer`
- **Behavior**: First settles accrued at old rate (inner AssetTransfer). Then updates salary_rate to new_rate. Resets last_withdrawal to now.
- **Retroactive settlement**: Guarantees employee gets everything owed at the previous rate before the change takes effect

#### `pause_stream(employee: Account) → None`
- **Caller**: Employer
- **Auth**: `Txn.sender == self.employer`
- **Behavior**: Settles accrued salary (inner AssetTransfer). Sets is_active = 0. Updates last_withdrawal to now.
- **Note**: Pause is reversible via `resume_stream()`

#### `milestone_pay(employee: Account, amount: UInt64) → None`
- **Caller**: Employer
- **Auth**: `Txn.sender == self.employer`
- **Behavior**: Sends a one-time payment of `amount` tokens to the employee via inner AssetTransfer. Independent of streaming — no rate or timestamp changes. Updates total_streamed.
- **Use case**: Pay for completed deliverables, bonuses, one-off tasks

#### `resume_stream(employee: Account) → None`
- **Caller**: Employer
- **Auth**: `Txn.sender == self.employer`
- **Behavior**: Sets is_active = 1. Resets stream_start and last_withdrawal to now (no retroactive accrual for paused period).
- **Validation**: Employee must exist and be currently paused

#### `remove_employee(employee: Account) → None`
- **Caller**: Employer
- **Auth**: `Txn.sender == self.employer`
- **Behavior**: Settles all accrued salary (inner AssetTransfer). Clears all local state. Decrements total_employees.
- **Final payout**: Employee receives everything owed before deregistration

#### `pause_all() → None`
- **Caller**: Employer
- **Auth**: `Txn.sender == self.employer`
- **Behavior**: Sets global is_paused = 1. All `withdraw()` calls will be rejected while paused. Does NOT settle individual streams (that would exceed inner transaction limits for many employees).
- **Use case**: Emergency stop — something is wrong, freeze everything

### Streaming Math
```
accrued_tokens = salary_rate × (current_timestamp - last_withdrawal) / 3600

Where:
  salary_rate    = tokens per hour (base units, 6 decimals)
  timestamps     = Unix epoch seconds from Global.latest_timestamp
  division       = integer division (no floating point on AVM)

Example:
  rate           = 1,000,000 (= 1.000000 PAYUSD/hr = $1/hr)
  elapsed        = 1800 seconds (30 minutes)
  accrued        = 1,000,000 × 1800 / 3600 = 500,000 (= $0.50)
```

### ASA Specification
| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Asset name | "AlgoFlow USD" |
| Unit name | "PAYUSD" |
| Decimals | 6 |
| Total supply | 1,000,000,000,000 (1M tokens in base units) |
| Default frozen | false |
| Manager | Employer address |
| Reserve | Employer address |
| Freeze | Employer address |
| Clawback | Contract address (enables programmatic transfers) |

### Algorand State Limits
| Resource | Limit | AlgoFlow Usage |
|----------|-------|---------------|
| Global state keys | 64 | 5 used (plenty of room) |
| Local state keys per account | 16 | 5 used per employee |
| Key name length | 64 bytes | All keys well under limit |
| UInt64 value size | 8 bytes | All values are UInt64 |
| Byte-slice value size | 128 bytes | Not used |
| Atomic group size | 16 transactions | Batch register up to 3 employees (3 app calls) |
| Inner transactions per group | 256 | Sufficient for all operations |
| Opcode budget | 700 base (pooled) | Simple math operations, well within budget |

## Frontend Specification

### Routes
| Route | Page | Purpose | Auth Required |
|-------|------|---------|--------------|
| `/` | Landing | "How it Works" diagram, role selection | No |
| `/employer` | Employer Dashboard | Fund, register, manage employees | Wallet connected + is employer |
| `/employee` | Employee Dashboard | View accrual, withdraw, history | Wallet connected |

### Component Architecture
```
App.tsx
├── Landing.tsx
│   ├── Silk.tsx (3D background)
│   ├── HowItWorks.tsx (architecture diagram)
│   └── RoleSelector.tsx
├── EmployerDashboard.tsx
│   ├── SetupChecklist.tsx (first-time guidance)
│   ├── ContractHealth.tsx (balance, runway, low-fund warning)
│   ├── EmployeeList.tsx
│   │   └── EmployeeRow.tsx (rate, status, accrued, actions)
│   ├── RegisterForm.tsx (address + rate input)
│   ├── FundForm.tsx (deposit amount)
│   ├── MilestonePayForm.tsx (per-employee)
│   └── EmergencyControls.tsx (pause all)
├── EmployeeDashboard.tsx
│   ├── StreamCounter.tsx (real-time accrual ticker)
│   ├── WithdrawButton.tsx
│   ├── RateDisplay.tsx (multi-unit: $/hr, $/day, $/week, $/month)
│   ├── StatusBadge.tsx (active/paused + "New" indicator)
│   └── TransactionHistory.tsx (with explorer links)
├── Shared/
│   ├── WalletConnect.tsx (provider abstraction)
│   ├── NetworkBadge.tsx (LocalNet/Testnet indicator)
│   ├── SpotlightCard.tsx (glassmorphism hover card)
│   ├── SpotlightCard.css
│   ├── ShinyText.tsx (shimmer gradient text)
│   ├── ShinyText.css
│   ├── Toast.tsx (react-hot-toast wrapper)
│   └── ExplorerLink.tsx (tx → Lora Explorer URL)
├── hooks/
│   ├── usePayrollContract.ts (contract interaction)
│   ├── useStreamAccrual.ts (client-side timer)
│   ├── useWallet.ts (wallet state)
│   └── useContractState.ts (polling global/local state)
├── lib/
│   ├── algorand.ts (Algod/Indexer client setup)
│   ├── constants.ts (app-wide configuration)
│   └── utils.ts (formatters, address truncation)
└── types/
    └── index.ts (TypeScript interfaces)
```

### Design System (AlgoGate-Inspired)

#### Color Palette
| Token | Value | Usage |
|-------|-------|-------|
| `--bg-dark` | `#0a0f0d` | Page background |
| `--surface` | `#fffdf8` | Card backgrounds |
| `--surface-dark` | `#111316` | Dark card backgrounds |
| `--primary` | `#137636` | Primary actions, headers |
| `--primary-dark` | `#0d5427` | Hover states |
| `--accent` | `#f25f6c` | Alerts, live indicators, warnings |
| `--stream-green` | `#5dcaa5` | Streaming counter, accrual display |
| `--text-primary` | `#1a1a1a` | Text on light surfaces |
| `--text-light` | `#f0f0f0` | Text on dark surfaces |

#### Typography
| Role | Font | Weight | Notes |
|------|------|--------|-------|
| Body | Geist Variable | 400 | System-ui fallback |
| Headings | Fraunces Variable | 600 | Serif, elegant, letter-spacing -0.03em |
| Monospace | ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular | 400 | Addresses, amounts, counters |

#### Effects
| Effect | CSS | Usage |
|--------|-----|-------|
| Glassmorphism | `backdrop-filter: blur(18px); border: 1px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.24); box-shadow: 0 28px 80px rgba(6,12,8,0.28)` | All cards |
| Spotlight | Radial gradient following `--mouse-x`, `--mouse-y` CSS custom properties | SpotlightCard hover |
| Shimmer | Gradient background-clip animation (200% width, 5s linear infinite) | Key text elements |
| Silk | Three.js fragment shader with procedural noise + time uniform | Full-page background |
| Stream tick | CSS counter animation with `transition: all 0.3s ease` | StreamCounter |

### Wallet Integration Architecture
```
@txnlab/use-wallet-react
├── KMD Provider (LocalNet)
│   ├── Host: http://localhost:4002
│   ├── Token: aaaa... (AlgoKit default)
│   └── Signing: server-side KMD daemon (no popup)
└── Pera Provider (Testnet)
    ├── WalletConnect protocol
    ├── ARC-0001 compliant
    └── Signing: Pera Wallet app (popup/mobile)

useWallet() hook returns:
  - activeAddress: string | null
  - providers: WalletProvider[]
  - signTransactions: (txns) => Promise<Uint8Array[]>
  - isConnected: boolean
```

### Role Detection Logic
```
1. User connects wallet → activeAddress available
2. Frontend queries contract global state for "employer" key
3. If activeAddress === employer → redirect to /employer
4. Else → redirect to /employee
5. Fallback: manual toggle on landing page
```

### Timezone Handling
- **On-chain**: All timestamps are Unix epoch seconds (UTC) from `Global.latest_timestamp`
- **Frontend**: `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone` detects browser timezone
- **Display**: All dates/times converted to local: "Mar 23, 2026 11:30 AM IST"
- **Storage**: Timezone preference cached in `localStorage`
- **Rate display**: No timezone impact — rates are per-hour regardless of timezone

---

## Demo Flow (9-Step Sequence)

This is the exact sequence to perform during the hackathon live demo (3 minutes):

| Step | Action | What Judges See | Algorand Feature Demonstrated |
|------|--------|----------------|------------------------------|
| 1 | Create PAYUSD token (ASA) | Token appears on explorer | ASA creation |
| 2 | Deploy AlgoFlow contract | Contract appears on explorer | ARC-4 smart contract |
| 3 | Fund contract with 100,000 PAYUSD | Balance updates on employer dashboard | Atomic transfer (fund + app call) |
| 4 | Register Employee A at $100/hr | Employee appears in list | Local state allocation |
| 5 | Register Employee B at $150/hr, Employee C at $80/hr | All 3 employees streaming | Atomic batch registration |
| 6 | Switch to Employee A dashboard | Counter ticking up in real-time | Inner transaction + math-based streaming |
| 7 | Employee A withdraws accrued salary | Tokens arrive in wallet, tx on explorer | Inner AssetTransfer, instant finality |
| 8 | Switch back to employer — update Employee B rate to $200/hr | Retroactive settlement + new rate applied | Atomic settlement + state update |
| 9 | Employer pauses Employee C | Stream stops, accrued settled | Pause with settlement |

**Bonus step (if time)**: Send milestone payment to Employee A — "Bonus for completing the smart contract audit"

### Demo Fallback Script
If the live demo fails, `scripts/demo.py` runs the entire flow in terminal:
```
$ python scripts/demo.py --network testnet
[1/9] Creating PAYUSD token... ✓ Asset ID: 789456
[2/9] Deploying AlgoFlow contract... ✓ App ID: 123456
[3/9] Funding contract with 100,000 PAYUSD... ✓ Tx: ABC123...
[4/9] Registering Employee A ($100/hr)... ✓
[5/9] Registering Employees B & C (batch)... ✓
[6/9] Waiting 30 seconds for accrual...
[7/9] Employee A withdrawing... ✓ Received 0.833333 PAYUSD
[8/9] Updating Employee B rate to $200/hr... ✓ Settled $1.25 at old rate
[9/9] Pausing Employee C... ✓ Settled $0.666666
Demo complete. All transactions verified on Algorand Testnet.
```

## Success Metrics

### Demo Metrics (Hackathon — Primary)
- Live streaming counter visibly ticking up during demo
- Successful on-chain withdrawal verified on Algorand Explorer
- Complete 9-step demo flow executes in under 3 minutes
- Zero crashes or error states during live demo
- Judges can click explorer links and verify transactions independently

### Technical Metrics
- Smart contract deployed and operational on Algorand Testnet
- 12 ABI methods callable and tested
- Frontend loads in under 2 seconds
- All transactions confirm within 5 seconds (Algorand finality)
- Automated demo script completes full flow without manual intervention
- pytest test suite passes with meaningful coverage

### Innovation Metrics
- First programmable payroll streaming protocol on Algorand (verified — no direct competitor)
- Demonstrates 4+ Algorand-unique features: ASAs, atomic transfers, inner transactions, instant finality
- Milestone-based payments alongside continuous streaming (dual-mode payroll)
- Rekeying demonstration (stretch) would add a 5th unique feature

## Tech Stack

| Layer | Technology | Version | Rationale |
|-------|-----------|---------|-----------|
| Smart Contracts | Algorand Python (algopy) | Latest via AlgoKit | Clean typed Python, ARC-4 compliant, modern Algorand standard |
| Tooling | AlgoKit CLI | 2.10.2 | Official Algorand toolchain — scaffolding, compilation, deployment, localnet |
| SDK | py-algorand-sdk (algosdk) | Latest | Transaction building, account management, indexer queries |
| Frontend | React 19 + TypeScript | 19.x | Best Algorand wallet ecosystem (@txnlab/use-wallet-react) |
| Build | Vite | 5.x+ | Fast dev server, HMR, TypeScript support |
| Styling | Tailwind CSS 4 | 4.x | Utility-first, matches AlgoGate aesthetic requirements |
| 3D/Animation | Three.js + Motion | Latest | Silk background, shimmer text effects |
| Wallet | Pera Wallet (WalletConnect) | Latest | Primary Algorand wallet, ARC-0001 compliant |
| Wallet (Dev) | KMD (AlgoKit default) | Bundled | LocalNet signing without Pera dependency |
| Network (Dev) | AlgoKit LocalNet | Docker-based | Instant blocks, no faucet needed, fast iteration |
| Network (Demo) | Algorand Testnet | Public | Judges can verify on explorer, real network behavior |
| Testing | pytest | Latest | Smart contract unit tests |
| Testing (FE) | Vitest | Latest | Frontend unit tests |
| Runtime | Python 3.14.3 | 3.14.3 | Already installed |
| Runtime | Node.js 22.14.0 | 22.14.0 | Already installed |

---

## Constraints

| Constraint | Impact | Mitigation |
|-----------|--------|------------|
| **48-hour build window** | Aggressive MVP scoping — 42 MVP FRs, 5 STRETCH | AI-assisted development, pre-built component patterns |
| **Solo builder** | No parallel human work | Heavy AI agent usage for code generation and testing |
| **First-time Algorand** | Sprint 0 must include learning buffer | AlgoKit scaffolding reduces boilerplate; Context7 docs available |
| **Demo is #1 priority** | Every decision optimizes for smooth live demo | Automated demo script as fallback; demo reset for clean reruns |
| **Pera Wallet + LocalNet** | Pera cannot connect to LocalNet directly | KMD provider for LocalNet dev, Pera for Testnet only (same useWallet hook) |
| **Testnet faucet limit** | 10 ALGO/day from Lora faucet | Deploy to Testnet once at the end, not iteratively |
| **Local state: 16 keys/account** | 5 keys used per employee — room for growth | Well within limits |
| **Atomic group: 16 txn max** | Batch registration capped at ~15 employees | Demo uses 3 employees — no issue |
| **No budget** | Free-tier tools only | Algonode public nodes, Testnet, local development |
| **AVM opcode budget** | 700 base, pooled across group | Simple math operations — well within budget |
| **Program size** | 8KB base + 3 extra pages | AlgoFlow contract expected to be well under limit |

---

## Timeline

| Sprint | Focus | Estimated Effort | Priority | Key Deliverables |
|--------|-------|-----------------|----------|-----------------|
| 0 | Scaffold | 3-4 hours | P0 | AlgoKit init, project structure, localnet running, dependencies installed, basic contract skeleton compiles |
| 1 | Smart Contract | 10-12 hours | P0 | 12 ABI methods implemented and tested, ASA creation script, deployment scripts, pytest suite |
| 2 | Employer Dashboard | 8-10 hours | P0 | Wallet connect, fund, register (batch), employee list, pause/resume, update rate, milestone pay, emergency controls, runway indicator |
| 3 | Employee Dashboard | 8-10 hours | P0 | Wallet connect, streaming counter, withdraw, history, role routing, landing page, demo script, demo reset |
| 4 | Polish & Deploy | 4-6 hours | P0 | Testnet deployment, design polish, UX states, timezone, stretch features if time |

**Total estimated: 33-42 hours** (fits in 48h window with buffer for debugging and unexpected issues)

### Sprint Dependencies
```
Sprint 0 ──→ Sprint 1 ──→ Sprint 2 ──→ Sprint 3 ──→ Sprint 4
(scaffold)   (contract)   (employer)   (employee)   (polish)
                │              │             │
                └──────────────┴─────────────┘
                Contract must be deployed
                before either dashboard works
```

---

## Key Decisions Log

| ID | Decision | Rationale | Date |
|----|----------|-----------|------|
| DEC-001 | Algorand Python (algopy) for contracts, not PyTeal | User preference. Cleaner syntax, typed, better AlgoKit integration. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-002 | AlgoKit CLI for tooling | Official Algorand toolchain. Scaffolding, compilation, deployment, localnet. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-003 | React 19 + Vite + Tailwind for frontend | Best Algorand wallet ecosystem. Matches AlgoGate aesthetic. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-004 | Frontend built AFTER smart contract infra | User preference. Contracts must be stable before dashboard can interact. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-005 | Project name: AlgoFlow | Short, memorable, ties to Algorand branding. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-006 | Two separate dashboards (employer + employee) | More impressive for judges — role-based thinking, dedicated UX. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-007 | Dark theme with AlgoGate SDK aesthetic | User preference. Glassmorphism, spotlight cards, Silk 3D background. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-008 | Automated demo script as fallback | Solo builder insurance — Python script runs full flow if live demo breaks. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-009 | Rekeying as bombshell STRETCH feature | Problem statement requires "delegated transaction authority." Unique to Algorand. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-010 | LocalNet for dev, Testnet for final demo | Faster iteration on LocalNet. Testnet for explorer verification by judges. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-011 | Milestone-based payments in MVP | User explicitly requested. Problem statement mentions "milestone-based." | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-012 | PAYUSD as stablecoin analog ($1 = 1 PAYUSD) | Dollar signs in UI — instantly understandable for judges. Like USDT. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-013 | @txnlab/use-wallet with KMD + Pera | Same useWallet() hook for both LocalNet and Testnet. Zero code changes. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-014 | Timezone handling (UTC on-chain, local in UI) | User wants robust application. Browser Intl API for detection. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-015 | Multi-unit rate display | $/hr, $/day, $/week, $/month shown simultaneously for employees. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-016 | resume_stream, remove_employee, pause_all promoted to MVP | User value audit found 3 CRITICAL gaps: irreversible pause, no emergency stop, no off-boarding. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-017 | Low-fund warning banner + setup checklist added | User value audit recommendations for employer UX. Low effort, high impact. | 2026-03-23 |
| DEC-018 | "New" badge for employee status changes | User value audit recommendation. localStorage-based last-visit comparison. | 2026-03-23 |

---

## Risk Register

| # | Risk | Likelihood | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|------|-----------|--------|------------|
| R1 | Algorand Python compiler bugs block contract development | Medium | High | Keep contract methods simple. Test each method immediately after writing. Have AlgoKit docs ready via Context7. |
| R2 | Testnet congestion during live demo | Low | Critical | Automated demo script as fallback. LocalNet as backup environment. |
| R3 | Pera Wallet connection fails during demo | Medium | High | KMD fallback available. Pre-connect wallet before demo starts. Test connection 5 minutes before. |
| R4 | Contract opcode budget exceeded | Low | High | AlgoFlow operations are simple math. Monitor budget during testing. |
| R5 | Employee opt-in UX friction adds steps to demo | Medium | Medium | Pre-opt-in employees in setup script. Show opt-in only if needed for narrative. |
| R6 | Rekeying complexity derails schedule | High | Medium | Classified as STRETCH. Only attempt after ALL MVP is complete and tested. |
| R7 | Frontend design polish takes too long | Medium | Medium | Port AlgoGate components early (Silk, SpotlightCard, ShinyText). They're self-contained. |
| R8 | MBR (Minimum Balance Requirement) miscalculation | Medium | High | Fund accounts generously in setup script. 10 ALGO per account is more than enough. |
| R9 | Indexer unavailable for transaction history | Low | Medium | Cache recent transactions in frontend state. History is nice-to-have for demo. |
| R10 | Demo exceeds 3-minute window | Medium | Medium | Practice the sequence. Use demo script for consistent timing. |

---

## Open Questions

1. **USDC vs custom token**: Demo uses custom PAYUSD token. Real-world version would use Algorand-native USDC (ASA ID 31566704 on Mainnet). No impact on demo.
2. **Rekeying demo scenario**: If attempted as STRETCH — employer rekeys a sub-account to HR manager address. HR manager calls contract methods using delegated authority. Exact flow TBD during Sprint 4 if time permits.
3. **Contract upgradability**: Current design is immutable after deployment. Acceptable for hackathon; production would need proxy pattern or versioned contracts.
4. **Multi-employer aggregation**: Alice (employee persona) works for 2-3 DAOs. Current design is single-contract. Multi-contract aggregation deferred to Vision — acknowledged as a known limitation.

---

## Competitive Positioning

AlgoFlow is the **first programmable payroll streaming protocol on Algorand**. No direct competitor exists on the chain.

### vs Sablier (Ethereum, 552K+ streams, ~$250M TVL)
| Dimension | Sablier | AlgoFlow |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| Finality | 12.8 minutes | 3.3 seconds |
| Cost per stream | $2-$45 gas | $0.00024 fixed |
| Batch operations | Sequential (each stream is separate tx) | Atomic groups (up to 16 in one batch) |
| Key management | No rekeying — new address = new setup | Native rekeying for delegated authority |
| Milestone payments | Not supported | Native milestone_pay() method |

### vs Superfluid (500+ projects, $9M raised)
| Dimension | Superfluid | AlgoFlow |
|-----------|-----------|----------|
| Token requirement | Must wrap ERC-20 to Super Token | ASAs stream natively — zero wrapping |
| Security track record | $8.7M exploit in 2022 | Simpler architecture, smaller attack surface |
| Streaming model | Constant Flow Agreement (complex) | Simple math: rate × time / 3600 |
| One-to-many | IDA (Instant Distribution Agreement) | Atomic group registration |

### vs LlamaPay (used by Curve, Yearn, Beefy)
| Dimension | LlamaPay | AlgoFlow |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| Chain support | EVM-only | Algorand (first on this chain) |
| Features | Basic streaming + indefinite streams | Streaming + milestone payments + pause/resume/remove |
| Gas efficiency | 3.2x cheaper than Sablier | ~10,000x cheaper than any EVM option |

### Unique to AlgoFlow
- **Milestone payments**: No competitor offers both continuous streaming AND one-time milestone payments in the same contract
- **Atomic batch operations**: Register multiple employees in a single indivisible transaction
- **Overdraft protection**: Automatic pause when funds are low — no failed withdrawals
- **Dual-mode payroll**: Time-based streaming + deliverable-based milestone payments

---

## Audit Trail

### P0 Audits Completed
1. **User Value Audit** (`docs/research/user-value-audit.md`) — Employer score: 9/10, Employee score: 9/10. Three STRETCH features promoted to MVP (resume, remove, pause_all). Two new FRs added (low-fund warning, status change badge).
2. **Docs Sync Audit** (`docs/research/docs-sync-audit.md`) — 17 inconsistencies found: 10 CRITICAL (stale counts), 5 WARNING (naming), 2 INFO (historical). All actionable items resolved.
3. **Ambiguity Resolutions** (`docs/research/ambiguity-resolutions.md`) — All 10 ambiguities answered, all 15 gaps addressed, all 5 contradictions resolved.

### Research Artifacts
- `docs/research/market-analysis.md` — Competitive landscape, hackathon patterns, market context
- `docs/research/technical-analysis.md` — Algorand Python, AlgoKit, algosdk, wallet integration, limitations
- `docs/research/requirements-gaps.md` — Requirements mapping, implicit features, FR list (note: some counts are historical — see ambiguity-resolutions for current values)
